claims.claim: We investigate whether a small learned confidence-triggered reread policy can improve the accuracy of a constrained-context question-answering system relative to answer-only training on partial context.

evidence_refs.quote: # Confidence-Triggered Reread Training: A Synthetic Study of Learned Reread Policies for Budget-Constrained Retrieval

claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: In a synthetic key/value retrieval task with distractor facts, a first-pass answerer operates on a reduced fact budget where the target fact may be absent.

evidence_refs.quote: # Confidence-Triggered Reread Training: A Synthetic Study of Learned Reread Policies for Budget-Constrained Retrieval

claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: Across five random seeds with 8,000 training, 2,000 validation, and 3,000 test examples each, the confidence-triggered reread training (CTRR) policy improved accuracy over the partial-context answer-only baseline by a mean of 8.48 percentage points (worst seed: 6.20 pp; best seed: 11.78 pp) at reread rates of 20–26%.

evidence_refs.quote: # Confidence-Triggered Reread Training: A Synthetic Study of Learned Reread Policies for Budget-Constrained Retrieval

claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: CTRR also outperformed matched-rate random rereading by a mean of 2.29 percentage points, indicating that the trigger learned useful confidence structure rather than merely spending additional context.

evidence_refs.quote: # Confidence-Triggered Reread Training: A Synthetic Study of Learned Reread Policies for Budget-Constrained Retrieval

claims.claim: This raises a policy question: can a system learn to identify low-confidence situations and selectively request more context, achieving better accuracy per unit of context consumed than naive strategies?

claims.claim: We define a Confidence-Triggered Reread Training (CTRR) policy that observes confidence features from a first-pass answerer operating on reduced context and decides whether to reread the full context before producing a final answer.

evidence_refs.quote: # Confidence-Triggered Reread Training: A Synthetic Study of Learned Reread Policies for Budget-Constrained Retrieval

claims.claim: We compare CTRR against (1) an answer-only baseline that never rereads, (2) a matched-rate random reread control that rereads at the same rate but without confidence conditioning, and (3) a full-context oracle.

claims.claim: The goal is not to demonstrate production viability but to isolate whether a learned confidence trigger can extract useful signal from first-pass uncertainty and convert it into accuracy gains under controlled conditions.

claims.claim: CTRR policy.** The same first-pass classifier produces an initial answer along with confidence features: maximum class probability, margin (difference between top-two class probabilities), entropy over the class distribution, and budget fraction ($b / b_{\text{full}}$).
